Summary: Refined the RouteGate settings sidebar so its navigation buttons are flush, full-width rows inside the panel rather than card-like pills. The sidebar now uses a full-bleed list with no internal gaps, and the active state is an inset accent instead of a separate bordered chip.

Tasks:
1. [finished] Confirm the settings sidebar remains readable and responsive after the row-style navigation change.

Affected files:
1. [modified] frontend/src/app/globals.css — Reworked the settings sidebar layout so buttons are flush, full-width rows with no extra gaps and no card styling. | Notes: Added full-bleed nav rows, removed rounded button cards, and switched the active treatment to an inset accent.
2. [inspected] frontend/src/features/settings/SettingsWorkspace.tsx — Verified the shell markup that wraps the sidebar and main content so the CSS change could stay localized. | Notes: No additional markup change was needed in this pass.

content:
Summary: Unified the settings sidebar and main content into a single connected shell in the RouteGate frontend. The settings workspace now uses one shared card container with an internal divider and full-width overflow clipping so the panels visually read as one integrated layout rather than separate cards.

Tasks:
1. [finished] Verify the settings shell still renders correctly after the composition and style changes.
2. [ongoing] Keep future dashboard shell changes in the composition layer rather than embedding layout concerns inside the settings panels.

Affected files:
1. [modified] frontend/src/features/settings/SettingsWorkspace.tsx — Adjusted the settings shell markup so the sidebar and content area share one card container instead of separate cards. | Notes: Removed per-panel card styling and kept the section content intact.
2. [modified] frontend/src/app/globals.css — Updated the settings workspace styles to remove the visual gap between panels and add an internal divider for the unified shell. | Notes: Made the sidebar/content relationship feel connected while preserving responsiveness.

content:
Summary: Investigated a Routes admin-page name mismatch where admin saw WAH4H/WAH4P drift. Root cause was a denormalized route.ownerName snapshot in MongoDB; the backend Routes list previously returned the stored snapshot directly. I changed the registry list path to re-resolve current owner names from the users collection on read, with stored ownerName as fallback. Added a unit test for owner-name fallback and documented the behavior in the backend README. Targeted backend tests for registry and api passed; full `go test ./...` still has a separate pre-existing registry test failure in destination tests.

Tasks:
1. [finished] Investigate and explain the WAH4H vs WAH4P mismatch in the Routes admin dashboard.
2. [finished] Update the backend route listing implementation to resolve owner names from users at read time.
3. [finished] Verify the change with targeted Go tests and record the remaining unrelated full-suite failure.

Affected files:
1. [modified] backend/internal/registry/store.go — Added user-based owner-name resolution in the route list path and preserved snapshot fallback logic. | Notes: Introduced a users collection reference, owner-name lookup helper, and display-name override conversion.
2. [modified] backend/internal/registry/store_test.go — Added coverage for the owner-name override/fallback behavior used by route listing. | Notes: Pure unit test for the public conversion helper.
3. [modified] backend/README.md — Documented that route list responses resolve current owner names from the users collection. | Notes: Clarifies denormalized snapshot behavior for future maintainers.

metadata.summary: Investigated a Routes admin-page name mismatch where admin saw WAH4H/WAH4P drift. Root cause was a denormalized route.ownerName snapshot in MongoDB; the backend Routes list previously returned the stored snapshot directly. I changed the registry list path to re-resolve current owner names from the users collection on read, with stored ownerName as fallback. Added a unit test for owner-name fallback and documented the behavior in the backend README. Targeted backend tests for registry and api passed; full `go test ./...` still has a separate pre-existing registry test failure in destination tests.

content:
Summary: Restructured the router maintenance page centering after repeated right-alignment complaints. The template now uses a dedicated fixed .viewport wrapper with inset 0 and grid place-items center, while main is a bounded centered column and heading lines center independently. This keeps 'is temporarily unavailable.' on one line without letting it shift the whole screen right.

Tasks:
1. [finished] Restructure the maintenance page so the fallback screen centers horizontally and vertically while preserving the no-wrap unavailable phrase.

Affected files:
1. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\RouteGate\router\internal\proxy\maintenance_page.go — Restructured maintenance page HTML and CSS to fix persistent right-alignment/centering issues. | Notes: Added .viewport wrapper, changed body overflow/min-height behavior, bounded main, and centered heading lines independently.

content:
Summary: Converted the admin users timestamp from last-login to last-active semantics. The backend now stores and refreshes lastActiveAt whenever an authenticated request resolves a user session, while preserving lastLoginAt as a compatibility fallback for older records. The frontend admin users table and mobile user cards now show Last active instead of Last login.

Tasks:
1. [finished] Verify backend session-resolution flow updates the user's last-active timestamp on authenticated access and returns the refreshed value in auth responses.
2. [finished] Verify the admin users table and mobile user cards display the new Last active label and use the updated frontend field.
3. [finished] Leave a note that repository-wide lint/test issues remain unrelated to this change and were not fixed here.

Affected files:
1. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\RouteGate\backend\internal\identity\store.go — Added lastActiveAt persistence, compatibility fallback serialization, and the TouchLastActive storage method used by authenticated access. | Notes: Also kept lastLoginAt as a deprecated alias in the public JSON contract.
2. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\RouteGate\backend\internal\api\handler.go — Refreshed the active timestamp on successful login, password-based session renewal, and every authenticated request that passes requireUser. | Notes: The handler now writes back the current active time into the in-memory user before returning auth responses.
3. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\RouteGate\frontend\src\features\users\types.ts — Renamed the managed user timestamp contract to lastActiveAt so the admin UI matches the new backend field. | Notes: The frontend type no longer depends on lastLoginAt.
4. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\RouteGate\frontend\src\features\users\UsersTable.tsx — Updated the admin users table and mobile cards to label and render Last active using the new timestamp field. | Notes: Kept the existing visual layout and added a neutral empty-state message for users with no activity yet.

metadata.summary: Converted the admin users timestamp from last-login to last-active semantics. The backend now stores and refreshes lastActiveAt whenever an authenticated request resolves a user session, while preserving lastLoginAt as a compatibility fallback for older records. The frontend admin users table and mobile user cards now show Last active instead of Last login.

content:
Summary: Added an admin-only account filter to the routes table so admins can filter routes by owner name, while keeping the existing Latest/A-Z sort. The selected owner filter persists in localStorage per signed-in admin and resets safely when invalid or cleared. Route workspace now passes current user identity into the table, and the filter logic was isolated into a small preferences helper.

Tasks:
1. [ongoing] Monitor for follow-up UI tweaks if admins want the current account option labeled differently, such as 'My account' versus the display name.

Affected files:
1. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\RouteGate\frontend\src\features\routes\RouteTable.tsx — Added admin-only owner filtering, persisted selection wiring, and empty-state clearing behavior in the routes table UI. | Notes: Also now accepts current user identity props from the workspace.
2. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\RouteGate\frontend\src\features\routes\RoutesWorkspace.tsx — Passed current user identity to the route table so filter persistence can be scoped per signed-in user and account labels can be rendered. | Notes: No backend changes were required.
3. [created] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\RouteGate\frontend\src\features\routes\routeTablePreferences.ts — Centralized localStorage keying and read/write helpers for the persisted account filter preference. | Notes: Keeps storage access out of the main table component.

The selected owner filter persists in localStorage per signed-in admin and resets safely when invalid or cleared. Route workspace now passes current user identity into the table, and the filter logic was isolated into a small preferences helper.
